---
title: "DEG_CTC"
author: "Sanga Mitra"
output: html_document
date: "2025-07-15"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## R Markdown

```{r libraries}
library(readxl)
library(WriteXLS)
library(writexl)
library(openxlsx)
library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(Matrix)
library(edgeR)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
library(VennDiagram)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(ggpubr)
library(forcats)
library(cowplot)
library(here)
```

```{r MSBB-full dataset load}

# Define regions
regions <- c("FP", "IFG", "PHG", "STG")

# Create a function 
load_region_data <- function(region) {
  list(
    Allgene = read.csv(here("data", "MSBB", paste0(region, "_all.csv"))),
    DTANGLE = read.csv(here("data", "MSBB", paste0(region, "_dtangle.csv"))),
    CIBER   = read.csv(here("data", "MSBB", paste0(region, "_Cibersort.csv")))
  )
}

# Load one region (e.g., FP) based on requirement
BR <- load_region_data("FP")

# Extract data for the loaded region
Allgene <- BR$Allgene
DTANGLE <- BR$DTANGLE
CIBER   <- BR$CIBER
```

```{r MSBB- common dataset load for Fig. S2}
# Define regions
regions <- c("FP134", "IFG134", "PHG134", "STG134")

# Create function
load_region_data <- function(region) {
  list(
    Allgene = read.csv(here("data", "MSBB", "common134", paste0(region, "_all.csv"))),
    DTANGLE = read.csv(here("data", "MSBB", "common134", paste0(region, "_dtangle.csv")))
  )
}

# Load one region
BR <- load_region_data("FP134")

# Extract objects
Allgene <- BR$Allgene
DTANGLE <- BR$DTANGLE
```

```{r MSBB metadata}
# Read metadata - FP
FP <- read.csv(here("data", "MSBB", "metadata", "metadata_PFC_FP.csv"))
# select pathology column
FP <- subset(FP, select = c(2, 43))

# Read metadata - IFG
IFG <- read.csv(here("data", "MSBB", "metadata", "metadata_inferior_frontal_gyrus.csv"))
# select pathology column
IFG <- subset(IFG, select = c(2, 43))

# Read metadata - PHG
PHG <- read.csv(here("data", "MSBB", "metadata", "metadata_parahippocampal_gyrus.csv"))
# select pathology column
PHG <- subset(PHG, select = c(2, 50))

# Read metadata - STG
STG <- read.csv(here("data", "MSBB", "metadata", "metadata_superior_temporal_gyrus.csv"))
# select pathology column
STG <- subset(STG, select = c(2, 43))

```

```{r Figure 2A & Fig. S2: representing cell type proportions}
# Cell type proportion data (STG used as an example here)
dtangleCTP <- read.csv(
  here("data", "MSBB", "cell_estimates",
       "bulkcounts_superior_temporal_gyrus_Dtangle_estimates_celltype_estimates.csv")
)
colnames(dtangleCTP)[1]<- "specimenID"

# add pathology column from metadata (for the region chosen, in this case: STG)
dtangleCTP <- inner_join(dtangleCTP, STG, by=c("specimenID"))

# Reshape the data from wide to long format for ggplot2
dtangleCTP_long <- melt(
  dtangleCTP, 
  id.vars = c("specimenID", "pathology"), 
  variable.name = "CellType", 
  value.name = "Proportion"
)

# box plot

# Rename pathology levels and reorder them
CTP_long <- dtangleCTP_long %>%
  mutate(pathology = recode(pathology, "NoAD" = "CTL")) %>%
  mutate(pathology = fct_relevel(pathology, "CTL", "AD"))  # Ensure CTL appears first

# Compute mean and standard error for each group
summary_data <- CTP_long %>%
  group_by(CellType, pathology) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_value = mean(Proportion, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_value = sd(Proportion, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Plot with correctly positioned error bars, renamed labels, and reordered categories
boxplot <- ggplot(CTP_long, aes(x = CellType, y = Proportion, fill = pathology)) +     
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA, color = "black", position = position_dodge(0.6)) +  # Dodge for clear separation
    geom_errorbar(data = summary_data, 
                  aes(x = CellType, y = mean_value, ymin = mean_value - se_value, ymax = mean_value + se_value, group = pathology),
                  width = 0.2, color = "black", position = position_dodge(0.6)) +  # Ensure error bars align with boxplots
    geom_point(data = summary_data, aes(x = CellType, y = mean_value, group = pathology), 
               color = "black", size = 2, shape = 21, fill = "white", position = position_dodge(0.6)) +  # Mean points
    labs(x = "", y = "Proportions")  +       
    scale_fill_manual(
        values = c(
            "CTL" = "blue1",  
            "AD" = "chocolate2"
        )
    ) +       
    theme_minimal() +  
    theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),  # Removes gridlines
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", face = "bold", size = 11),  
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", face = "bold", size = 12),  
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", face = "bold", size = 14),  
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.5),
        legend.text = element_text(color = "black", size= 12),
        legend.title = element_text(color = "black", size= 12)
    )

# Display the plot
print(boxplot)

# save the plot 
region <- "STG" # Change region for other regions

ggsave(
  filename = here("figures", paste0("dtangle_CTPboxplot_", region, ".pdf")),
  plot = boxplot,
  height = 4,
  width = 6,
  units = "in"
)


```

```{r Figure 2A, Fig.S2 & Table S1: calcualte the significance in difference btw cell proportions of CTL and AD}
# Rename file
CTP <- dtangleCTP

# Ensure pathology is a factor with correct reference order
CTP$pathology <- factor(CTP$pathology, levels = c("NoAD", "AD"))

# Function to calculate p-values and effect sizes for each cell type
calculate_p_values <- function(data, cell_types, group_col) {

  results <- lapply(cell_types, function(cell_type) {

    # Define groups explicitly: Control (NoAD) as reference
      group_ctl <- na.omit(data[[cell_type]][data[[group_col]] == "NoAD"])
      group_ad  <- na.omit(data[[cell_type]][data[[group_col]] == "AD"])

    # Two-sided Wilcoxon rank-sum test
    test_result <- wilcox.test(
      group_ctl,
      group_ad,
      alternative = "two.sided",
      exact = FALSE
    )

    # Rank-biserial correlation (effect size)
    n1 <- length(group_ctl)
    n2 <- length(group_ad)
    U  <- as.numeric(test_result$statistic)

    # Effect size:
    # Negative -> lower in AD
    # Positive -> higher in AD
    effect_size <- 1 - (2 * U) / (n1 * n2)

    data.frame(
      Cell_Type   = cell_type,
      P_Value     = test_result$p.value,
      Effect_Size = effect_size
    )
  })

  # Combine per-cell-type results
  results_df <- do.call(rbind, results)

  # Adjust p-values using Benjamini–Hochberg correction
  results_df$Adjusted_P <- p.adjust(results_df$P_Value, method = "BH")

  # Function to assign significance levels (based on adjusted p-values)
  get_significance <- function(p) {
    if (p <= 0.001) {
      "***"
    } else if (p <= 0.01) {
      "**"
    } else if (p <= 0.05) {
      "*"
    } else {
      "ns"
    }
  }

  # Apply significance function
  results_df$Significance <- sapply(results_df$Adjusted_P, get_significance)

  return(results_df)
}

# Define cell type columns
cell_types <- c("Neurons", "Astrocytes", "Oligodendrocytes", "Microglia")

# Calculate p-values and effect sizes
p_values_result <- calculate_p_values(CTP, cell_types, "pathology")

# Print results
print(p_values_result)

```

```{r Fig. S1A: compare cell type proportions- dtangle vs CIBERSORT}
# STG shown here. Replace brain region as required
# dtangle cell estimates
dtangleCTP <- read.csv(
  here("data", "MSBB", "cell_estimates",
       "bulkcounts_superior_temporal_gyrus_Dtangle_estimates_celltype_estimates.csv")
)
colnames(dtangleCTP)[1]<- "specimenID"

# CIBERSORT cell estimates
CiberCTP <- read.csv(
  here("data", "MSBB", "cell_estimates",
       "bulkcounts_superior_temporal_gyrusCIBERSORT_estimates_celltype_estimates.csv")
)
colnames(CiberCTP)[1]<- "specimenID"

# add pathology column from metadata
dtangleCTP <- inner_join(dtangleCTP, STG, by=c("specimenID"))
CiberCTP <- inner_join(CiberCTP, STG, by=c("specimenID"))

# Melt the data into long format
dtangle_long <- melt(dtangleCTP, variable.name = "CellType", value.name = "Proportion")
dtangle_long$Dataset <- "dtangle"
Ciber_long <- melt(CiberCTP, variable.name = "CellType", value.name = "Proportion")
Ciber_long$Dataset <- "CIBERSORT"

# Combine the two datasets
# CIBER and Dtangle

merged_data <- merge(Ciber_long, dtangle_long, 
                     by = c("specimenID", "pathology","CellType"), 
                     suffixes = c("_Ciber", "_dtangle"))

# Rename pathology level
merged_data$pathology <- recode(merged_data$pathology,
                                "NoAD" = "CTL")

# Calculate correlation for each cell type
correlations <- merged_data %>%
  group_by(CellType,pathology) %>%
  do({
    cor_value <- cor(.$Proportion_dtangle, .$Proportion_Ciber, use = "complete.obs")
    data.frame(correlation = round(cor_value, 2))
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

# Print correlation for each cell type
print(correlations)


# Ensure correct order: CTL first, then AD
merged_data$pathology <- factor(merged_data$pathology,
                                levels = c("CTL", "AD"))

correlations$pathology <- factor(correlations$pathology,
                                 levels = c("CTL", "AD"))

# Plot scatter plots for each cell type with customization
scp <- ggplot(merged_data,
              aes(x = Proportion_Ciber,
                  y = Proportion_dtangle,
                  color = CellType)) +
    
    geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
    
    # Correlation labels (color-coded)
    geom_text(data = correlations,
              aes(x = 0.05,
                  y = 0.98 - as.numeric(factor(CellType)) * 0.07,
                  label = paste0("r = ", round(correlation, 2)),
                  color = CellType),
              inherit.aes = FALSE,
              hjust = 0,
              vjust = 1,
              show.legend = FALSE) +
    
    labs(x = "CIBERSORT Proportions",
         y = "dtangle Proportions",
         color = "Cell Type") +
    
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1),
                    ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
       "Neurons" = "Blue",
  "Astrocytes" = "Orange",
  "Oligodendrocytes" = "Purple"
    )) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 10, angle = 45),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 10),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", face = "bold", size = 14),
        legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    
    # Stack vertically
    facet_wrap(~ pathology)

print(scp)

# save the plot
region <- "STG" # Change region for other regions

ggsave(
  filename = here("figures", paste0(region, "_dtanglevsCiber_CTP.pdf")),
  plot = scp,
  height = 4,
  width = 6,
  units = "in"
)

```

```{r Figure 2B & Fig. S3: Overlap with individual brain marker genes}
# Load the Marker files
Neuron <- read.csv(here("data", "markers", "Neurons.csv"))
Astro  <- read.csv(here("data", "markers", "Astrocytes.csv"))
Micro  <- read.csv(here("data", "markers", "Microglia.csv"))
Oligo  <- read.csv(here("data", "markers", "Oligodendrocytes.csv"))

# Load gene id file
Allids <- read.csv(here("data", "markers", "merged_gene_mapping.csv"))

# Matched for gene symbols
Markernew <- left_join(Oligo, Allids, by=c("ensembl_id"))

colnames(Markernew)[3] <- "HGNC_symbol"

# Overlap with DEGs (load from MSBB folder)
noCTC <- inner_join(Allgene, Markernew, by=c("HGNC_symbol"))
noCTC <- subset(noCTC , select = c(7,1,5))

dtangle <- inner_join(DTANGLE, Markernew, by=c("HGNC_symbol"))
dtangle <- subset(dtangle , select = c(7,1,5))

compare <-inner_join(noCTC, dtangle, by=c("HGNC_symbol"))

colnames(compare) <- c("HGNC_symbol","noCTC_LogFC", "noCTC_adjpval","CTCDT_LogFC","CTCDT_adjpval" )

# adpval
compare <- subset(compare , select = c(1,3,5))
colnames(compare) <- c("HGNC_symbol","noCTC","CTCDT")

# Convert data to long format
data_long <- melt(compare, id.vars = "HGNC_symbol")

# Perform hierarchical clustering on rows
row_dendrogram <- hclust(dist(compare[, -1]))  # Compute hierarchical clustering
row_order <- row_dendrogram$order  # Get the order of genes after clustering

# Reorder the Gene factor based on clustering
data_long$HGNC_symbol <- factor(data_long$HGNC_symbol, levels = compare$HGNC_symbol[row_order])

# Create heatmap with row clustering- adjpvalue
data_long <- data_long %>%
    mutate(fill_color = ifelse(value <= 0.05, "darkorange1", "cornsilk2"))

p1 <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = variable, y = HGNC_symbol, fill = fill_color)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_identity() +  # Step 3: use exact colors as defined
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 1)) +  # Keep gene names visible
    labs(x = "", y = "Genes") +
    theme(
        plot.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1)
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c("noCTC" = "noCTC", "CTCDT" = expression("CTC"^"DT"))) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 1.5, color = "gray", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 1.5, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2)

print(p1)

# save the plot
region <- "STG" # Change region for other regions

ggsave(
  filename = here("figures", paste0(region, "_compareOligoMarkersFDR.pdf")),
  plot = p1,
  height = 2,
  width = 2,
  units = "in"
)

# LogFC
compare <-inner_join(noCTC, dtangle, by=c("HGNC_symbol"))

colnames(compare) <- c("HGNC_symbol","noCTC_LogFC", "noCTC_adjpval","CTCDT_LogFC","CTCDT_adjpval" )

compare <- subset(compare , select = c(1,2,4))
colnames(compare) <- c("HGNC_symbol","noCTC","CTCDT")

# Convert data to long format
data_long <- melt(compare, id.vars = "HGNC_symbol")

# Use the same row order
# Reorder the Gene factor based on clustering
data_long$HGNC_symbol <- factor(data_long$HGNC_symbol, levels = compare$HGNC_symbol[row_order])

# Create heatmap with row clustering- LogFC

p2 <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = variable, y = HGNC_symbol, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 1)) +  # Keep gene names visible
  labs(x = "", y = "Genes") + theme(
    plot.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1) ) +
  coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("noCTC" = "noCTC", "CTCDT" = expression("CTC"^"DT"))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1.5, color = "gray", linewidth = 0.3)  

print(p2)

# save the plot
region <- "STG" # Change region for other regions

ggsave(
  filename = here("figures", paste0(region, "_compareOligoMarkersLogFC.pdf")),
  plot = p2,
  height = 2,
  width = 2,
  units = "in"
)
```

```{r Table 2: Find significant genes}

# Load region specific data from MSBB folder

bulknoctc <- subset(Allgene, Allgene$adj_pval<=0.05)
dtangle <- subset(DTANGLE, DTANGLE$adj_pval<=0.05)

noCTC_dtangle <- inner_join(bulknoctc, dtangle, by= c("HGNC_symbol"))
ciber_dtangle <- inner_join(ciber, dtangle, by= c("HGNC_symbol"))

# saving is optional
write_xlsx(bulknoctc,
           here("results", "noCTC.xlsx"))

write_xlsx(dtangle,
           here("results", "CTC.xlsx"))

```

```{r Table S3: noCTCexcl vs CTCDTexcl stringent cutoff}
Allgene <- subset(Allgene , select = c(7,1,4,5))
DTANGLE <- subset(DTANGLE , select = c(7,1,4,5))

Joined <- inner_join(Allgene, DTANGLE, by=c("HGNC_symbol"))
colnames(Joined) <- c("HGNC_symbol","LogFC_noCTC","pval_noCTC","adjp_noCTC","LogFC_CTC","pval_CTC","adjp_CTC")

noCTCexcl <- subset(Joined, Joined$adjp_noCTC<=0.05 & Joined$pval_CTC>0.1)
CTCDTexcl <- subset(Joined, Joined$adjp_CTC<=0.05 & Joined$pval_noCTC>0.1)

write_xlsx(noCTCexcl,"/Users/sanga/Desktop/IIT Madras/DEG_CTC/MSBB/MSBB_newcovariates/noCTCexcl.xlsx")
write_xlsx(CTCDTexcl,"/Users/sanga/Desktop/IIT Madras/DEG_CTC/MSBB/MSBB_newcovariates/CTCDTexcl.xlsx")
```

```{r Figure 3A & Fig. S1B: scatter plot for method comparison using all genes}
# Filter all genes and corresponding logFC from 3 files (load data from MSBB folder)
Bulk <- dplyr::select(Allgene, HGNC_symbol, logFC)
Ciber <-dplyr::select(DTANGLE, HGNC_symbol, logFC)
Dtangle <- dplyr::select(CIBER, HGNC_symbol, logFC)

# Merge the data frames by gene_name for all genes
merged_data <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "HGNC_symbol"), 
                      list(Ciber, Dtangle,Bulk))

# Rename columns for clarity for allgene data
colnames(merged_data) <- c("gene_name", "LogFC_Ciber", "LogFC_dtangle", "LogFC_noCTC")


# Step 3: Create scatter plots to compare ranks between datasets

# Bulk vs. CIBERSORT
plot1 <- ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = LogFC_noCTC, y = LogFC_Ciber)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = expression("noCTC vs CTC"^"CS"),
    x = "LogFC in noCTC",
    y = expression("LogFC in CTC"^"CS")) +
  theme_minimal() +  
    theme(
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 12, angle = 0),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black",  size = 14)
    )

print(plot1)

# Bulk vs. DTANGLE
plot2 <- ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = LogFC_noCTC, y = LogFC_dtangle)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, color = "green") +
  labs(x = "LogFC in noCTC",
       y = expression("LogFC in CTC"^"DT")) +
  theme_minimal() +  
    theme(
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black",  size = 14)
    )

print(plot2)

# CIBERSORT vs. DTANGLE
plot3 <- ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = LogFC_Ciber, y = LogFC_dtangle)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, color = "red") +
  labs( x = expression("LogFC in CTC"^"CS"),
        y = expression("LogFC in CTC"^"DT")) +
  theme_minimal() +  
    theme(
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black",  size = 14)
    )

print(plot3)

# save the plot
region <- "STG" # Change region for other regions

plots <- list(
  noCTC_vs_CTCCS = plot1,
  noCTC_vs_CTCDT = plot2,
  CTCCS_vs_CTCDT = plot3
)

for (name in names(plots)) {
  ggsave(
    filename = here("figures", paste0(region, "_", name, ".pdf")),
    plot = plots[[name]],
    height = 5,
    width = 6,
    units = "in"
  )
}


```

```{r Fig. S1C: Bland-Altman Plot}
# Filter all genes and corresponding LogFC  (load data from MSBB folder)
Ciber <- dplyr::select(CIBER, HGNC_symbol, logFC)
Dtangle <- dplyr::select(DTANGLE, HGNC_symbol, logFC)

# Merge the data frames by gene_name for all genes
merged_data <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "HGNC_symbol"), list(Ciber, Dtangle))

colnames(merged_data) <- c("gene_name", "LogFC_Ciber", "LogFC_dtangle")

# Calculate Bland-Altman statistics
bland_altman_data <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    mean_diff = (LogFC_Ciber + LogFC_dtangle) / 2, # Mean of methods
    diff = LogFC_Ciber - LogFC_dtangle,            # Difference
    sd_diff = sd(diff)                                       # Standard deviation of differences
  )

# Compute the mean difference (bias) and confidence interval
mean_bias <- mean(bland_altman_data$diff)
ci_bias <- t.test(bland_altman_data$diff)$conf.int  # Confidence interval

# Bland-Altman Plot
baplot <- ggplot(bland_altman_data, aes(x = mean_diff, y = diff)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +  # Scatter plot points
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean_bias, linetype = "solid", color = "red", linewidth = 1.2) +  # Mean bias line
  geom_hline(yintercept = ci_bias[1], linetype = "dotted", color = "gray", linewidth = 1) +  # Lower CI
  geom_hline(yintercept = ci_bias[2], linetype = "dotted", color = "gray", linewidth = 1) +  # Upper CI
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "blue", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed") +  # Loess regression
  labs( x = bquote("Mean"),
        y = bquote("Difference")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),  # Remove gridlines
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 12)
  )

# Print plot
print(baplot)

# save the plot

region <- "STG" # Change region for other regions

comparison <- "CTCCS_vs_CTCDT"

ggsave(
  filename = here("figures", paste0(region, "_BlandAltman_", comparison, ".pdf")),
  plot = baplot,
  height = 5,
  width = 6,
  units = "in"
)

```

```{r Fig. S4: concordance scatter plot with bar plot for flip type Insert}
# Extract column- gene name and LogFC value (load data from MSBB folder)
Bulk <-  dplyr::select(Allgene, HGNC_symbol, logFC)
Dtangle <- dplyr::select(DTANGLE, HGNC_symbol, logFC)

# Merge the data frames by gene_name for all genes
merged_data <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "HGNC_symbol"), 
                      list(Dtangle,Bulk))

# Rename columns for clarity for allgene data
colnames(merged_data) <- c("gene_name", "LogFC_dtangle", "LogFC_noCTC")

# Select LogFC columns
cor_matrix <- cor(merged_data[, c("LogFC_dtangle", "LogFC_noCTC")],
                  method = "pearson")

# Classify flip types
df <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(flip_type = case_when(
    LogFC_noCTC > 0 & LogFC_dtangle < 0 ~ "Up to Down",
    LogFC_noCTC < 0 & LogFC_dtangle > 0 ~ "Down to Up",
    TRUE ~ "Concordant"
  ))

# Count flipped types
flip_counts <- df %>%
  filter(flip_type != "Concordant") %>%
  count(flip_type)

# Main concordance plot
p_main <- ggplot(df, aes(x = LogFC_noCTC, y = LogFC_dtangle, color = flip_type)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Up to Down" = "red", "Down to Up" = "blue", "Concordant" = "lemonchiffon4")) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
    labs(x = expression("LogFC in noCTC"),
         y = expression("LogFC in CTC"^"DT"),
         color = "Flip Type") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        legend.position = "none",  # Adjust legend position inside plot
        legend.key = element_rect(fill = NA),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.5), 
        legend.text = element_text(color = "black", size= 14),
        legend.title = element_text(color = "black", size= 14)
    )

# Bar plot of flipped counts
p_bar <- ggplot(flip_counts, aes(x = flip_type, y = n, fill = flip_type)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.4) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Up to Down" = "red", "Down to Up" = "blue")) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
    theme(
        legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.background = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5), 
        legend.text = element_text(color = "black", size= 10),
        legend.title = element_text(color = "black", size= 10)
    )

# Combine with inset
final_plot <- ggdraw() +
    draw_plot(p_main) +
    draw_plot(p_bar, x = 0.65, y = 0.2, width = 0.3, height = 0.3) 

# Display
print(final_plot)

# save the plot
region <- "STG" # Change region for other regions

ggsave(
  filename = here("figures", paste0(region, "_flipplot.pdf")),
  plot = final_plot,
  height = 5,
  width = 6,
  units = "in"
)


```

```{r Figure 3B: Density Plot: Compare Distribution of LogFC}
# Extract column- gene name and LogFC value (load data from MSBB folder)
Bulk <-  dplyr::select(Allgene, HGNC_symbol, logFC)
Dtangle <- dplyr::select(DTANGLE, HGNC_symbol, logFC)

# Merge the data frames by gene_name for all genes
merged_data <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "HGNC_symbol"), 
                      list(Dtangle,Bulk))

# Rename columns for clarity for allgene data
colnames(merged_data) <- c("gene_name", "LogFC_dtangle", "LogFC_noCTC")

# Reshape data for ggplot
df_long <- reshape2::melt(merged_data,
                          id.vars = "gene_name",
                          measure.vars = c("LogFC_dtangle", "LogFC_noCTC"),
                          variable.name = "Correction",
                          value.name = "LogFC")

# Density plot
plotd <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = LogFC, fill = Correction)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) + coord_cartesian(xlim = range(df_long$LogFC)) +
    labs(
        x = "Log2 Fold Change",
        y = "Density"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
        values = c("darkorange", "steelblue"),
        labels = c( expression(CTC^DT),"noCTC"),
        name = NULL  # Remove legend title
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        legend.key = element_rect(fill = NA),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 12, angle = 0),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black",  size = 14)
    )

print(plotd)

# save the plot
region <- "STG" # Change region for other regions

ggsave(
  filename = here("figures", paste0(region, "_densityplot.pdf")),
  plot = plotd,
  height = 5,
  width = 6,
  units = "in"
)

```

```{r Fig. S5, S9: Venn Diagram- Overlap and significance}
# noCTC vs CTCDT
bulknoctc <- subset(Allgene, Allgene$adj_pval<=0.05)
dtangle <- subset(DTANGLE, DTANGLE$adj_pval<=0.05)

# Extract gene names from each tab
genes_dtangle <- dtangle$HGNC_symbol
genes_bulknoctc <- bulknoctc$HGNC_symbol


# Combine all genes from the three lists and find unique entries
all_genes <- unique(c( genes_dtangle, genes_bulknoctc))


# Bulk and dtangle
venn <- venn.diagram(
    x = list(
        CTCDT = genes_dtangle,
        noCTC = genes_bulknoctc
    ),
    category.names = c(expression("CTC"^"DT"), "noCTC"),
    filename = NULL,  # Save plot in memory instead of a file
    fill = c("darkorange", "steelblue"),
    alpha = 0.5,
    cex = 2.5, cat.cex = 2.5,
    fontface = "bold",
    cat.fontface = "bold",
    main = ""
)

# Save the Venn diagram
region <- "FP" # Change region for other regions
png(
  here("figures", paste0(region, "_venn_noCTC_vs_CTCDT.png")),
  width = 400,
  height = 400
)
grid.draw(venn)
dev.off()

# Overlap and significance

# Calculate the total number of unique genes
total_genes_in_universe <- length(unique(Allgene$HGNC_symbol))

# Function to calculate overlap significance
calculate_hypergeo_pval <- function(list1, list2, universe_size) {
  overlap <- length(intersect(list1, list2))  # Number of overlapping genes
  size_list1 <- length(list1)  # Size of list1
  size_list2 <- length(list2)  # Size of list2
  
  # Hypergeometric test
  p_value <- phyper(overlap - 1, size_list1, universe_size - size_list1, size_list2, lower.tail = FALSE)
  
  return(list(overlap = overlap, p_value = p_value))
}

# Calculate overlaps and p-values for each pair
result_dtangle_noCTC <- calculate_hypergeo_pval(genes_dtangle, genes_bulknoctc, total_genes_in_universe)

# Print results
cat("Total unique genes in the universe:", total_genes_in_universe, "\n")
cat("Overlap and significance:\n")
cat("dtangle vs noCTC: Overlap =", result_dtangle_noCTC$overlap, 
    ", P-value =", result_dtangle_noCTC$p_value, "\n")


```

```{r Figure 4A: region-wise DEG consistency before and after deconvolution- Jaccard}
# reassign names for each region  (load data from MSBB folder)
BM10_pre <- Allgene
BM10_post <- DTANGLE
BM44_pre <- Allgene
BM44_post <- DTANGLE
BM36_pre <- Allgene
BM36_post <- DTANGLE
BM22_pre <- Allgene
BM22_post <- DTANGLE

# Define Function to Extract DEG Genes
get_deg_genes <- function(deg_table, fdr_thresh = 0.05) {
  deg_table <- deg_table[!is.na(deg_table$adj_pval), ]
  deg_table$HGNC_symbol[deg_table$adj_pval < fdr_thresh]
}

# Create DEG Lists by Region
deg_list_pre <- list(
  FP = get_deg_genes(BM10_pre),
  IFG = get_deg_genes(BM44_pre),
  PHG = get_deg_genes(BM36_pre),
  STG = get_deg_genes(BM22_pre)
)

deg_list_post <- list(
  FP = get_deg_genes(BM10_post),
  IFG = get_deg_genes(BM44_post),
  PHG = get_deg_genes(BM36_post),
  STG = get_deg_genes(BM22_post)
)

# Compute Jaccard & Overlap Coefficient

compute_overlap_metrics <- function(deg_list) {
  regions <- names(deg_list)
  n <- length(regions)
  jaccard <- overlap <- matrix(NA, nrow = n, ncol = n,
                               dimnames = list(regions, regions))
  
  for (i in seq_along(regions)) {
    for (j in seq_along(regions)) {
      g1 <- deg_list[[i]]
      g2 <- deg_list[[j]]
      inter <- length(intersect(g1, g2))
      union <- length(union(g1, g2))
      min_set <- min(length(g1), length(g2))
      
      jaccard[i, j] <- inter / union
      overlap[i, j] <- inter / min_set
    }
  }
  list(Jaccard = jaccard, Overlap = overlap)
}

metrics_pre <- compute_overlap_metrics(deg_list_pre)
metrics_post <- compute_overlap_metrics(deg_list_post)


# Plot JSI heatmaps

# Split heatmap
# Combine pre and post Jaccard matrices into one
combined_jaccard <- matrix(NA, nrow = 4, ncol = 4)
rownames(combined_jaccard) <- colnames(combined_jaccard) <- names(deg_list_pre)

for (i in 1:4) {
    for (j in 1:4) {
        if (i > j) {
            combined_jaccard[i, j] <- metrics_pre$Jaccard[i, j]  # Lower triangle: pre-correction
        } else if (i < j) {
            combined_jaccard[i, j] <- metrics_post$Jaccard[i, j] # Upper triangle: post-correction
        } else {
            combined_jaccard[i, j] <- 1  # Optionally leave diagonal blank
        }
    }
}

# Custom color gradient
split_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("white", "lightgreen", "forestgreen"))(100)

# Plot the split matrix and save it
pdf(
  here("figures", "Split_Jaccard_Heatmap_Pre_vs_Post.pdf"),
  width = 5,
  height = 4
)

pheatmap(
    combined_jaccard,
    main = "Split Jaccard Index\nLower: Pre-Correction | Upper: Post-Correction",
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_cols = FALSE,
    color = split_colors,
    legend = TRUE,
    display_numbers = TRUE,
    fontsize_number = 12,
    number_color = "black",
    angle_col = 0
)
dev.off()


```

```{r Figure 4B: Heatmap for GSEA result}
# Load processed GSEA results generated from WebGestalt
data <- read.csv(
  here("data", "MSBB", "GSEA","merged_GSEAfull_results.csv"))

# Count number of GO and Reactome Functions

data %>%
  group_by(Function) %>%
  summarise(unique_geneSet_count = n_distinct(geneSet))

# Define color palette
color_palette <- colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 9, name = "RdBu")))(100)

# Generate separate heatmaps per Function for geneSet having atleast one FDR <=0.05

for (func in unique(data$Function)) {
    print(paste("Processing Function:", func))  # Debugging
    
    # Filter for current Function
    sub_data <- data %>% filter(Function == func)
    
    # Remove geneSets where all FDR values are > 0.05
    significant_geneSets <- sub_data %>%
        group_by(geneSet) %>%
        filter(any(FDR <= 0.05)) %>%  # Keep only if at least one entry has FDR <= 0.05
        ungroup()
    
    # Proceed only if there are geneSets left
    if (nrow(significant_geneSets) > 0) {
        print(paste("Total geneSets retained for", func, ":",      n_distinct(significant_geneSets$geneSet)))
        
        # Prepare data for heatmap
        heatmap_data <- significant_geneSets %>%
            dplyr::select(geneSet, Datatype, NES) %>%
            spread(key = Datatype, value = NES)
        
        row.names(heatmap_data) <- heatmap_data$geneSet
        sub_matrix <- as.matrix(heatmap_data[, -1])  # Remove geneSet column
        
        # Handle NA values (replace with 0)
        sub_matrix[is.na(sub_matrix)] <- 0
        
        # Convert all values to numeric
        sub_matrix <- apply(sub_matrix, 2, as.numeric)
        
        outfile <- here(
      "figures",
      paste0("Heatmap_",
             gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", func),
             ".pdf")
    )
        print(paste("Saving to:", outfile))
        
        pdf(file = outfile, width = 6, height = 5)
        
        pheatmap(
            sub_matrix,
            cluster_rows = TRUE,
            cluster_cols = TRUE,
            scale = "row",
            color = color_palette,
            main = paste("Heatmap for Function:", func),
            fontsize_row = 8,
            fontsize_col = 10
        )
        
        dev.off()
        
        print("Device closed")
    } else {
        print(paste("Skipping Function:", func, "since no geneSet meets FDR <= 0.05 criteria"))
    }
}

```

```{r Table S6: AD risk loci from Open Targets}
# File paths
input_file  <- here("data", "GWAS_OpenTargets", "OpenTarget_AD.tsv")
output_file <- here("data", "GWAS_OpenTargets", "AD_GWAS_genes_OpenTargets.tsv")

# Read Open Targets file for AD
ot <- read.delim(input_file, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Convert 'No data' to NA and coerce to numeric
ot <- ot %>%
  mutate(
    gwasCredibleSets = as.numeric(
      ifelse(gwasCredibleSets == "No data", NA, gwasCredibleSets)
    )
  )

# Sanity check (optional)
summary(ot$gwasCredibleSets)

# Extract AD GWAS risk genes 
ad_gwas_genes <- ot %>%
  filter(!is.na(gwasCredibleSets), gwasCredibleSets >= 0.2) %>%
  select(symbol, gwasCredibleSets) %>%
  arrange(desc(gwasCredibleSets)) %>%
  distinct()

# Inspect top genes
head(ad_gwas_genes, 10)

# Write output
write.table(
  ad_gwas_genes,
  file = output_file,
  sep = "\t",
  row.names = FALSE,
  quote = FALSE
)

# Processed AD risk file (converted .tsv file into .xlsx file)
# Already processed in previous step. So no need to process every time.
# The processed file already stored in folder. So can be used directly.

ADrisk <- read_xlsx(
  here("data", "GWAS_OpenTargets", "AD_GWAS_genes_OpenTargets.xlsx"),
  sheet = 1
)

# Merge Function
merge_ADrisk_DE <- function(ADrisk, DEdf, prefix) {
  DEdf %>%
    select(HGNC_symbol, logFC, PValue) %>%   # keep only relevant columns
    inner_join(ADrisk %>% select(HGNC_symbol), by = "HGNC_symbol") %>%  # merge by gene symbol
    rename(
      !!paste0(prefix, "_logFC") := logFC,
      !!paste0(prefix, "_PValue") := PValue
    )
}

# Merge ADrisk with DE datasets (load individual DEG file from MSBB)

ADrisk_pval_FPnoCTC <- merge_ADrisk_DE(ADrisk, Allgene, "FPnoCTC")
ADrisk_pval_FPCTC   <- merge_ADrisk_DE(ADrisk, DTANGLE, "FPCTC")

ADrisk_pval_IFGnoCTC <- merge_ADrisk_DE(ADrisk, Allgene, "IFGnoCTC")
ADrisk_pval_IFGCTC   <- merge_ADrisk_DE(ADrisk, DTANGLE, "IFGCTC")

ADrisk_pval_PHGnoCTC <- merge_ADrisk_DE(ADrisk, Allgene, "PHGnoCTC")
ADrisk_pval_PHGCTC   <- merge_ADrisk_DE(ADrisk, DTANGLE, "PHGCTC")

ADrisk_pval_STGnoCTC <- merge_ADrisk_DE(ADrisk, Allgene, "STGnoCTC")
ADrisk_pval_STGCTC   <- merge_ADrisk_DE(ADrisk, DTANGLE, "STGCTC")

# function: calculate adjusted p-values (FDR)

adjust_pval <- function(df, pcol, new_col) {
  df %>% mutate(!!new_col := p.adjust(.data[[pcol]], method = "BH"))
}

# Calculate adjusted p-values

regions <- c("FP", "IFG", "PHG", "STG")
conditions <- c("noCTC", "CTC")

for (r in regions) {
  for (cond in conditions) {
    
    obj_name <- paste0("ADrisk_pval_", r, cond)
    
    if (exists(obj_name)) {
      
      assign(
        obj_name,
        adjust_pval(
          get(obj_name),
          paste0(r, cond, "_PValue"),
          paste0(r, cond, "_adjP")
        )
      )
    }
  }
}

# Filter significant genes (adjP <= 0.05) for each dataset

for (r in regions) {
  for (cond in conditions) {
    
    source_obj <- paste0("ADrisk_pval_", r, cond)
    target_obj <- paste0("sig_genes_", r, cond)
    adj_col    <- paste0(r, cond, "_adjP")
    
    if (exists(source_obj)) {
      
      assign(
        target_obj,
        get(source_obj) %>%
          dplyr::filter(.data[[adj_col]] <= 0.05)
      )
    }
  }
}


# Create summary table for significant AD risk gene count

sig_counts <- expand.grid(
  Region = regions,
  Condition = conditions,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    Dataset = paste0(Region, Condition),
    Num_Significant = {
      obj_name <- paste0("sig_genes_", Region, Condition)
      if (exists(obj_name)) nrow(get(obj_name)) else NA
    }
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(Dataset, Num_Significant)


# Print the summary
print(sig_counts)

# Find common significant genes
common_sig_results <- list()

for (r in regions) {
  
  obj_noCTC <- paste0("sig_genes_", r, "noCTC")
  obj_CTC   <- paste0("sig_genes_", r, "CTC")
  
  if (exists(obj_noCTC) && exists(obj_CTC)) {
    
    common_sig_results[[r]] <- inner_join(
      get(obj_noCTC),
      get(obj_CTC),
      by = "HGNC_symbol"
    )
    
    cat("Region:", r,
        "| Common genes:", nrow(common_sig_results[[r]]), "\n")
  }
}


```

```{r Figure 5A: ROSMAP vs MSBB}
# Load data
# MSBB
FP_pre  <- read.csv(here("data","MSBB","FP_all.csv"))
FP_post <- read.csv(here("data","MSBB","FP_dtangle.csv"))

# Store everything in structured object

deg_data <- list(
  pre = list(
    MSBB   = FP_pre,
    ROSMAP = DLPFC_pre
  ),
  post = list(
    MSBB   = FP_post,
    ROSMAP = DLPFC_post
  )
)

# Function to compute overlap, significant genes and Venn Input

compute_overlap <- function(dataset_type) {
  
  MSBB   <- deg_data[[dataset_type]][["MSBB"]]
  ROSMAP <- deg_data[[dataset_type]][["ROSMAP"]]
  
  # Keep only common genes
  common <- inner_join(MSBB, ROSMAP, by = "HGNC_symbol")
  
  # Significant at FDR 5%
  sig_MSBB   <- common %>% filter(adj_pval.x <= 0.05)
  sig_ROSMAP <- common %>% filter(adj_pval.y <= 0.05)
  
  list(
    common= common,
    genes_MSBB   = sig_MSBB$HGNC_symbol,
    genes_ROSMAP = sig_ROSMAP$HGNC_symbol
  )
}

# Loop over both data sets to save both Venn simultaneously

for (dataset_type in c("pre", "post")) {
    
    overlap_genes <- compute_overlap(dataset_type)
    
    venn_obj <- venn.diagram(
        x = list(
            ROSMAP = overlap_genes$genes_ROSMAP,
            MSBB   = overlap_genes$genes_MSBB
        ),
        category.names = c("DLPFC", "FP"),
        filename = NULL,
        fill = c("steelblue", "lightpink"),
        alpha = 0.5,
        cex = 2,
        cat.cex = 2,
        cat.pos = c(-20, 20),
        cat.dist = c(0.03, 0.03)
    )
    
    # Save the plot
    pdf(
  file = here("figures",
              paste0("ROSMAP_MSBB_", dataset_type, "_FDR5.pdf")),
  width = 6,
  height = 5
)

grid.draw(venn_obj)
dev.off()

}


# overlap significance function
compute_enrichment <- function(universe, subset1, subset2, overlap) {
  # hypergeometric p-value (>= overlap)
  p_hyper <- phyper(overlap - 1, subset1, universe - subset1, subset2, lower.tail = FALSE)
  
  # contingency table
  a <- overlap
  b <- subset1 - overlap
  c <- subset2 - overlap
  d <- universe - (a + b + c)
  mat <- matrix(c(a, b, c, d), nrow = 2,
                dimnames = list(subset1 = c("yes", "no"),
                                subset2 = c("yes", "no")))
  fisher_res <- fisher.test(mat, alternative = "greater")
  
  expected <- subset1 * subset2 / universe
  fold_enrich <- overlap / expected
  
  list(
    expected_overlap = expected,
    fold_enrichment = fold_enrich,
    odds_ratio = fisher_res$estimate,
    p_value = fisher_res$p.value,
    hyper_p_value = p_hyper
  )
}

# calculate the significance for both dataset
for (dataset_type in c("pre", "post")) {
    
    overlap_genes <- compute_overlap(dataset_type)
    
    # Universe = genes tested in both cohorts
    universe <- nrow(overlap_genes$common)
    
    subset1 <- length(overlap_genes$genes_MSBB)
    subset2 <- length(overlap_genes$genes_ROSMAP)
    
    overlap <- length(intersect(
        overlap_genes$genes_MSBB,
        overlap_genes$genes_ROSMAP
    ))
    
    res <- compute_enrichment(universe, subset1, subset2, overlap)
    
    cat("\nDataset:", dataset_type, "\n")
     
    cat("Universe:", universe, "\n")
    cat("Subset1:", subset1, "\n")
    cat("Subset2:", subset2, "\n")
    cat("Overlap:", overlap, "\n\n")
    
    print(res)
}


```

```{r Figure 5B: ROSMAP vs MSBB severity score}
# Load metadata
meta <- read.csv(
  here("data", "DLPFC_FP_combinedmetadata.csv"),
  header = TRUE
)

# Select required columns
meta <- subset(meta, select = c(8, 9, 5, 6))

# Reshape to long format
meta_long <- reshape2::melt(
  meta,
  variable.name = "severity",
  value.name = "Score"
)

# Recode and reorder pathology
meta_long <- meta_long %>%
  mutate(pathology = recode(pathology, "NoAD" = "CTL")) %>%
  mutate(pathology = forcats::fct_relevel(pathology, "CTL", "AD"))

# Plot
box_plot <- ggplot(meta_long,
                   aes(x = pathology,
                       y = Score,
                       fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Severity") +
  facet_wrap(~severity) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "black", face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "FP" = "lightpink",
    "DLPFC" = "steelblue"
  ))

print(box_plot)

# Save plot (GitHub-friendly)
ggsave(
  filename = here("figures", "FPvsDLPFC_severity.pdf"),
  plot = box_plot,
  height = 5,
  width = 6,
  units = "in"
)
```
